description:
You are acting as a build-automation engineer who must prepare a per-user build environment that is portable across CI runners lacking root access.  
Carry out all steps strictly inside /home/user and **do not use sudo**.  
When you are done, the system must be in the exact state described below.

Goal  
1. Create deterministic, file-based configuration of
   • the POSIX TZ variable (timezone)  
   • the locale (LANG and LC_ALL variables)  

2. Produce an audit report confirming that the two settings work together.  
   The report must contain a *reproducible* timestamp so that the automated test
   can verify the result without depending on the real clock.

Required directory layout (create any missing directories):  
/home/user/build/  
/home/user/build/config/  
/home/user/build/logs/

Required files and their final contents  

A) /home/user/build/config/timezone.conf  
   └── Single line, no trailing spaces:  
       TZ=America/New_York  

B) /home/user/build/config/locale.conf  
   └── Two lines, each terminated with a single newline character:  
       LANG=en_US.UTF-8  
       LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8  

C) /home/user/.build_env  
   • If the file does **not** yet exist, create it.  
   • Append the following three lines (preserve any pre-existing content):  
       # >>> build environment <<<  
       source /home/user/build/config/timezone.conf  
       source /home/user/build/config/locale.conf  
     End the block with a newline.  
   The automated tests will grep for the exact marker text “# >>> build environment <<<”.

D) /home/user/build/logs/setup_report.log  
   This log proves that the configuration can be sourced in any shell.  
   Write exactly three lines, terminated with Unix newlines and **nothing else**:

       2020-12-31T19:00:00-05:00
       Locale: en_US.UTF-8
       Timezone: America/New_York

   • The first line is an ISO-8601 time-stamp representing the Unix epoch
     1609459200 (2021-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) *converted* to the
     America/New_York zone.  
     Hint: you can compute it with `date -d @1609459200 …`, but the task
     will be automatically checked only for the literal string above.  
   • No extra spaces or blank lines are allowed.

Verification rules the test suite will apply  
1. All paths exist with the exact names given (case-sensitive).  
2. timezone.conf contains exactly “TZ=America/New_York\n”.  
3. locale.conf contains exactly two lines as specified, each ending in `\n`.  
4. .build_env contains the three new lines in the given order somewhere in the file.  
5. setup_report.log exists and its **entire** content is the three lines shown, including the newline after the third line.  

Perform whatever shell actions you need (touch, echo, printf, etc.) to achieve this final state.
truth:
Pre-existing state  
• /home/user exists and is writable.  
• No other assumptions.

Final state required for a pass  

1. Directories  
   /home/user/build/ (mode 755)  
   /home/user/build/config/ (mode 755)  
   /home/user/build/logs/ (mode 755)

2. Files and contents (byte-exact)

–– /home/user/build/config/timezone.conf  
   0x0000  54 5A 3D 41 6D 65 72 69 63 61 2F 4E 65 77 5F 59 6F 72 6B 0A              ; TZ=America/New_York\n

–– /home/user/build/config/locale.conf  
   0x0000  4C 41 4E 47 3D 65 6E 5F 55 53 2E 55 54 46 2D 38 0A                      ; LANG=en_US.UTF-8\n
   0x0010  4C 43 5F 41 4C 4C 3D 65 6E 5F 55 53 2E 55 54 46 2D 38 0A                ; LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8\n

–– /home/user/.build_env  
   Existing content (if any) plus the following suffix:  
   # >>> build environment <<<\n
   source /home/user/build/config/timezone.conf\n
   source /home/user/build/config/locale.conf\n

–– /home/user/build/logs/setup_report.log  
   0x0000  32 30 32 30 2D 31 32 2D 33 31 54 31 39 3A 30 30 3A 30 30 2D 30 35 3A 30 30 0A 4C 6F 63 61 6C 65 3A 20 65 6E 5F 55 53 2E 55 54 46 2D 38 0A 54 69 6D 65 7A 6F 6E 65 3A 20 41 6D 65 72 69 63 61 2F 4E 65 77 5F 59 6F 72 6B 0A

   Interpreted text:  
   2020-12-31T19:00:00-05:00\n
   Locale: en_US.UTF-8\n
   Timezone: America/New_York\n

No superfluous characters, spaces, or lines are permitted.
